issue_title: Support encrypted GPG private key
issue_body:
Maybe I missed something, but currently there seem to be no way for sops to decrypt secrets in an environment, where no X-Server is present, if the GPG private key is encrypted.

This certainly works like expected in a GUI enviroment, where `/usr/bin/pinentry-gnome3` or similar programs can handle the GPG passphrase dialog. In a non-gui environment (CI), sops might need to call the gpg command with `--pinentry-mode loopback`  for it to accept input via STDIN.

I think that this should work:

```bash
echo "{GPG_PASSPHRASE}" | sops -d values-prod-enc.yaml
```

issue_title: Sometimes sops store broken data key (invalid key size).
issue_body:
After a few key rotations sops can leave encrypted file in a broken state.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Generate new encrypted file with single PGP key:
`sops -pgp <fingerprint> tmp.yml`
2) Launch a loop that constantly rotates data key: for bash
`while [[ $? == 0 ]]; do; sops -r -i tmp.yml; done`
3) Wait until the loop fails with error:

```
[PGP]	 INFO[0000] Decryption succeeded                          fingerprint=<fingerprint>
[SOPS]	 INFO[0000] Data key recovered successfully              
Error decrypting tree: Error walking tree: Could not decrypt value: crypto/aes: invalid key size 31
```

It usually takes 5-10 cycles to break the file.

This behaviour is observed in version 3.0.0 (in linux version downloaded from [releases](https://github.com/mozilla/sops/releases/download/3.0.0/sops-3.0.0.linux), as well as in darwin version installed by `brew install sops`).

PGP agent:
```
$ gpg --version
gpg (GnuPG) 2.1.21
libgcrypt 1.7.7
```

Example of decrypted data key from broken file:

```
$ cat tmp.yml | ruby -ryaml -e 'puts YAML.load(STDIN.read)["sops"]["pgp"][0]["enc"]' | gpg -d | hexdump -C
00000000  9c 74 86 a4 54 81 72 e1  92 9f f5 80 1d d0 c4 92  |.t..T.r.........|
00000010  9e 39 b7 dc 00 93 5f d7  3e 15 3f 78 13 69 3d     |.9...._.>.?x.i=|
0000001f
```

issue_title: Provide backoff context managers
issue_body:
I sometimes have a third-party function or method that I would like to retry using backoff. Currently, I have to wrap the call in a single line function and decorate that. It would be nice if I could, instead, use a context manager.

So what I currently do is:

```py
def do_a_thing(...):
    @backoff.on_exception(...)
    def _actually_do_third_party_thing():
        _do_third_party_thing()

    some_setup()
    _actually_do_third_party_thing()
    some_teardown()
```

And what I'd like to be able to do:

```py
def do_a_thing(...):
    some_setup()
    with backoff.on_exception(...):
        _do_third_party_thing()
    some_teardown()
```

issue_title: BoolParameters without config_path set to default value by set_global_parameters
issue_body:
Since my latest upgrade, my snakebite has stopped working. Looking into it, I see that before set_global_parameters is called, snakebite_autoconfig is True and after set_global_parameters is called, snakebite_autoconfig becomes False, overriding the value from client.cfg and causing the wrong snakebite client to be used.

The problem seems to be that the command-line argument is overriding the value from client.cfg, but for boolean flags the command-line argument is present and False when not specified. This bug affects any BoolParameter that doesn't specify a config_path, as Parameter.value is called to find the default value and this doesn't pass a task_name or param_name to _get_value, causing it to ignore client.cfg.

It looks like snakebite_autoconfig, use_snakebite, and record_task_history are the only current parameters vulnerable to this bug. parallel_scheduling is ok because it has config_path set.

issue_title: Random seed duplication in the PMF loss distribution sampling
issue_body:
The random seed for the sampling method implemented for the discrete probability distribution is reset to the same value for each ground motion field (see snippet below). If all gmfs are identical (eg. if the median gmf is sampled multiple times), this ends up sampling the same loss ratios for each gmf. Ideally, different values should be sampled from the discrete loss ratio distribution for each gmf, even if the gmfs are identical. 

```python
@DISTRIBUTIONS.add('PM')
class DiscreteDistribution(Distribution):
    seed = None  # to be set

    def sample(self, loss_ratios, probs):
        ret = []
        r = numpy.arange(len(loss_ratios))
        for i in range(probs.shape[1]):
            random.seed(self.seed)
            # the seed is set inside the loop to avoid block-size dependency
            pmf = stats.rv_discrete(name='pmf', values=(r, probs[:, i])).rvs()
            ret.append(loss_ratios[pmf])
        return ret
```

https://github.com/gem/oq-risk-tests/tree/master/test/scenario_risk/inputs/case_1h with `truncation_level = 0` provides a test case.

issue_title: Avoid the confusion between `oq engine --version` and `oq --version`
issue_body: `oq engine --version` should be renamed `oq engine --dbversion`. At least two people got confused by this.

issue_title: "New version released" warning
issue_body:
We should add a warning, in the command line and in the webui, that notifies users that a new OQ Engine release is available (for stable releases). This:
 - Can be disabled via confing (`openquake.cfg`)
 - Should silently fail if no connection is available
 - Should not create delays: a cache should be used (a field in the DB is enough...)

In the past this issue was less problematic since everyone was using Linux packages that get updated with `apt` or `yum`. Nowadays most people are using installers which need manual upgrade.

Releases can be retrieved from here https://developer.github.com/v3/repos/releases/

issue_title: IAM AssumeRolePolicyDocument Service ordering inconsistency
issue_body:
It appears that boto3 does not consistently preserve the order of an IAM Role's AssumeRolePolicyDocument's services as defined in a role's "Trusted Entities" in the AWS console.

To repro, create a role in the AWS console that can be assumed by multiple services, say `ec2.amazonaws.com` and `ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com`. Then run the following script a handful of times and notice that the order of the printed list is not consistent.
```python
import boto3
client = boto3.client('iam')
role = client.get_role(RoleName='MyRole')
print(role['Role']['AssumeRolePolicyDocument']['Statement'][0]['Principal']['Service'])
```

```
$ python test.py
['ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com', 'ec2.amazonaws.com']
$ python test.py
['ec2.amazonaws.com', 'ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com']
```

Is this expected behavior? We're using boto3 to define roles and policies in a git repo, but this inconsistency is resulting in more git diffs than we would expect. It would be great if boto3 always returned the same order, especially considering that it is of type `list` and not `set` which implies ordering.

issue_title: Using boto3 via ntml proxy
issue_body:
When uploading to S3 using a standard HTTP proxy, tshark shows me something similar to: 

<source> -> <proxy> HTTP 638 PUT http://s3-eu-west-xxxxxx.jpg HTTP/1.1 
<proxy> -> <source> HTTP 115 HTTP/1.1 100 Continue 
<proxy> -> <source> HTTP 600 HTTP/1.1 200 OK

And all works fine

When I do the same thing via an http proxy using NTLMS proxy, I get: 

<source> -> <proxy>  HTTP 727 PUT http://s3-eu-west-xxxxxx.jpg HTTP/1.1 , NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE
<proxy> -> <source> HTTP 1052 HTTP/1.1 407 Proxy Authorization Required , NTLMSSP_CHALLENGE (text/html)
<source> -> <proxy>  HTTP 907 PUT http://s3-eu-west-xxxxxx.jpg HTTP/1.1 , NTLMSSP_AUTH, User: <username>
<proxy> -> <source> HTTP 128 HTTP/1.1 100 Continue 
<proxy> -> <source> HTTP 509 HTTP/1.1 200 OK 

On the face of it, from that trace, all looks good - however in this second case, although the upload appears to succeed, boto3 returns:

botocore.exceptions.ConnectionClosedError: Connection was closed before we received a valid response from endpoint URL

Any ideas as to why this is...?

In documentation, it mentions when creating/updating auto scaling groups, we can pass a a list of subnets for the VPCZoneIdentifier key (http://boto3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/reference/services/autoscaling.html#AutoScaling.Client.update_auto_scaling_group)

In reality, it only accepts a single string:

response = asg_client.update_auto_scaling_group(
   ....:                AutoScalingGroupName=asg_name,
   ....:                LaunchConfigurationName=new_launch_configuration_name,
   ....:                VPCZoneIdentifier=['subnet-XXXXX','subnet-XXXXXX'],
   ....:                )

Invalid type for parameter VPCZoneIdentifier, value: ['subnet-XXXXXX', 'subnet-XXXX'], type: <type 'list'>, valid types: <type 'basestring'>

issue_title: Incorrect Error Handling in Flask
issue_body:
The code in flask/middleware.py has two issues that are related to how flask handles errors.

## Issue 1
According to Flasks design, the signal handler for `got_request_exception` gets executed *before* any error handling logic. This is an issue, because the middleware will process and finish the span as an error, even though an error handler might process the exception into a let's say `404` or even `200`. Unfortunately, the way the code is written, by the time the handler for `request_finished` gets called (which is *after* error handling), the span has already been processed and marked as finished.

## Issue 2
Related to above, the current code will always set the status code to `500`, which leads to exceptions that are handled and turned into a non-500 response to be wrongly reported in DataDog as `500`s even though they might have turned into something else.

## Potential Fix
A potential fix is to let the handler for `got_request_exception` only set the exception info on the active span and then let the handler for `request_finished` do the rest (incl. setting the error flag based on the final http status code).

See [this gist for a draft of a possible fix](https://gist.github.com/patforna/a3f61a3d17174622d29e2f741674bc2b).

Any other way to obtain that information?

issue_title: Proposal: expose ABCs in `backports_abc` namespace
issue_body:
I currently use the following conditional import (in Tornado) to get the generator type for `isinstance()` checks:

```
try:
    from collections.abc import Generator as GeneratorType  # py35+
except ImportError:
    from types import GeneratorType
```

This doesn't work on py27 with `backports_abc`, since the ABCs live in `collections` instead of `collections.abc`. I need to add another attempt to the conditional (`from collections import Generator as GeneratorType`), but since the only time this import would ever succeed is when `backports_abc` is present, I'd rather just do `from backports_abc import Generator`. 

If this pattern were adopted by all users of `backports_abc`, the `patch()` function would become unnecessary (but could still be useful on py3.x, where it installs the ABCs where a backport-unaware caller would expect to find them).

Specifically, I would do something like this in `backports_abc.py` for each patchable type:
```
try:
    Generator = _collections_abc.Generator
except AttributeError:
    Generator = mk_gen()
```

issue_title: Allow doubling a particular instance method on all instances of a class.
Native support for Tornado futures
issue_body:
Currently, if a method being doubled is an asynchronous operation, we use `tornado.gen.maybe_future` to wrap values into futures, and for exceptions for asynchronous operations, we use this:

    def failed_future(exc):
        future = tornado.gen.Future()
        future.set_exception(exc)
        return future

It would be great if doubles supported futures natively. The API could possibly look like,

    allow(foo).bar.and_raise.async(Exception('foo'))
    allow(foo).bar.and_return.async(result)

Doubles won't have to depend on Tornado for this and can just use the futures from `concurrent.futures` since Tornado supports those too.

issue_title: Allow custom locators to define a default criteria
issue_body:
In [elementfinder](https://github.com/rtomac/robotframework-selenium2library/blob/ea384de3885b905a306c12de50d788c5a2a14ae1/src/Selenium2Library/locators/elementfinder.py#L125) we have functionality to have certain criteria to automatically use a default locator if met. Our current implementation allows the specification of `//` to automatically use an xpath locator. It would be good if custom locators could define this functionality. 

The limitation of this is that it must not match any of the locator names. I.E. if a locator `list` is defined, `li` couldn't be used as a default locator criteria.

issue_title: Compatible releases are not compatible with Semantic Versions less than 1.0.0.
issue_body:
According to [PEP440](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440/#semantic-versioning),
> The "Major.Minor.Patch" (described in this PEP as "major.minor.micro") aspects of semantic versioning (clauses 1-9 in the 2.0.0-rc-1 specification) are fully compatible with the version scheme defined in this PEP

Also, 
> A compatible release clause consists of the compatible release operator ~= and a version identifier. It matches any candidate version that is expected to be compatible with the specified version.

According to [Semantic Versioning clause 4](http://semver.org/#spec-item-4),
> Major version zero (0.y.z) is for initial development. Anything may change at any time. The public API should not be considered stable.

By this logic, I would not expect `pkgdep ~= 0.1` to be satisfied, for example, by 0.2.0, since there is no guarantee that 0.2.0 will be backwards-compatible with 0.1.x; however, this seems to be the current behavior when using the compatible release operator in `install_requires`.

Strictly speaking, I'm not even sure `pkgdep ~= 0.1.0` should imply 0.1.1, although I suspect that is divergent from de facto expectations.

issue_title: Importing setuptools.dist executes a subprocess (8.0.x)
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **tonyandrewmeyer (Bitbucket: [tonyandrewmeyer](http://bitbucket.org/tonyandrewmeyer), GitHub: [tonyandrewmeyer](http://github.com/tonyandrewmeyer))**

---

I'm not sure if you'll consider this a bug or not (your call, of course).

In 8.0 and 8.0.1 (and I assume 8.0.2, although I haven't checked) importing setuptools.dist imports setuptools.windows_support.  windows_support has a method hide_file, which is wrapped by a decorator windows_only, which calls platform.system().  platform.system() (at least in 2.7.8) calls platform.uname(), which (on some platforms) uses _syscmd_uname to get the processor information, which calls os.popen (for uname).

So what this means is that just importing setuptools.dist will result in a subprocess running uname (on various platforms, including Debian 7).

Philosophically, I don't really like an import executing a subprocess.  However, this caused issues for me, because I have processes that do not have permission to create child processes.  So this means that importing setuptools.dist results in an IOError ([Errno 10] No child processes).

It would be great if the pre 8.0 behaviour was restored where this didn't happen.  For example, the hide_file method could determine if it was running on Windows when called rather than when imported.

---
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/pypa/setuptools/issue/297

issue_title: Remove dependency on wheel
issue_body: Currently the `setuptools.command.dist_info` command relies on the `bdist_wheel` code to create a `.dist-info` directory. But since wheel has no public API, this is dangerous as that code could go away in the next release. The `dist_info` command should be natively implemented in setuptools instead.

issue_title: Add VEP annotation
issue_body:
(extracted from another issue)

---
Sarah:
add an annotation column (for example: intronic; exonic-nonsynonymous; exonic-splice; exonic-synonynmous; intergenic; 5' UTR; 3' UTR)

---
Peter:
1. Would you want VEP or snpEff or something else?  Is it okay if PheWeb only gives users one choice?

Annotation: I want to be quite careful not to introduce problems into `sites.tsv`, since `matrixify` has to parse it.  Check version numbers of VEP/snpEff, and then have PheWeb manage everything. If VEP or snpEff can add exactly the annotations I want to a vcf-like file, this should be easy– I just annotate `sites.tsv`, pass along all columns into `augmented_phenos_gz/*.gz` and `matrix.tsv.gz`, convince my API to load and serve the annotations, and set up my default templates to render whatever's available.

---
Sarah:
VEP only is fine.

Annotation: VEP can write into a VCF file (--vcf flag) http://useast.ensembl.org/info/docs/tools/vep/script/vep_options.html#opt_vcf
You can can filter the VCF output http://useast.ensembl.org/info/docs/tools/vep/script/vep_filter.html

---
Peter:
VEP adds this to the end of `INFO`:

> ;CSQ=T|stop_gained|HIGH|CCT8L2|ENSG00000198445|Transcript|ENST00000359963|protein_coding|1/1||||1354|1094|365|W/*|tGg/tAg|||-1||HGNC|15553,<more copies>

documented by this header:

> ##INFO=<ID=CSQ,Number=.,Type=String,Description="Consequence annotations from Ensembl VEP. Format: Allele|Consequence|IMPACT|SYMBOL|Gene|Feature_type|Feature|BIOTYPE|EXON|INTRON|HGVSc|HGVSp|cDNA_position|CDS_position|Protein_position|Amino_acids|Codons|Existing_variation|DISTANCE|STRAND|FLAGS|SYMBOL_SOURCE|HGNC_ID">

in pairs:

> Allele : T
Consequence : stop_gained
IMPACT : HIGH
SYMBOL : CCT8L2
Gene : ENSG00000198445
Feature_type : Transcript
Feature : ENST00000359963
BIOTYPE : protein_coding
EXON : 1/1
INTRON :
HGVSc :
HGVSp :
cDNA_position : 1354
CDS_position : 1094
Protein_position : 365
Amino_acids : W/*
Codons : tGg/tAg
Existing_variation :
DISTANCE :
STRAND : -1
FLAGS :
SYMBOL_SOURCE : HGNC
HGNC_ID : 15553

From this, I would display `consequence: stopgained` in tables and maybe use `IMPACT: HIGH` to color Manhattan/LZ.  Most of the rest could get dumped at the bottom of the PheWAS page.  If I'm happy with the way `SYMBOL` and `Feature` are decided, I could use those instead of my gene annotation.

As I read the VEP annotation from a VCF, I'll take the most deleterious `consequence` and the highest `IMPACT`.  Then assert that `CSQ["alt"] == alt`.

Later: Figure out whether we can run [`ensembl-vep`](https://github.com/Ensembl/ensembl-vep) on `sites.tsv`, or if not what I must add.  Maybe add an empty `INFO` column or rename `rsids` to `ID`.

issue_title: get_bucket_policy() doesn't return correct output in one case
issue_body:
The following code shows a bug in  get_bucket_policy()  after setting two bucket policies with different prefixes. 

```
from minio import Minio
from minio.policy import Policy
from minio.error import ResponseError

client = Minio('s3.amazonaws.com', secure=True, access_key='xxx', secret_key='xxx/xxx/xxx')
bucket_name = 'mybucket'

try:
    client.set_bucket_policy(bucket_name, '', Policy.WRITE_ONLY)
    client.set_bucket_policy(bucket_name, 'public/', Policy.READ_ONLY)
    print(client.get_bucket_policy(bucket_name))
    print(client.get_bucket_policy(bucket_name, 'public/'))
except ResponseError as err:
    print(err)
```

The code above returns the following output:
```
none
readonly

issue_title: Overriding default global settings
issue_body:
I was wondering if there's an official way of overriding the default global settings. It seems like it's possible to do by directly setting a value on `dateparser.conf.settings`, like so:

```python
import dateparser
dateparser.conf.settings.RETURN_AS_TIMEZONE_AWARE = True
```

For my particular use case, I'd like for `dateparser` to always return timezone-aware datetimes, and it's a bit of a hassle to always pass in the setting to every call to `dateparser.parse()`. If my code snippet above is the preferred way of overriding default settings, I'd be more than happy to write up some documentation for it.

issue_title: Formats passed in date_formats only work for English, even if languages=["es"]
issue_body:
Apparently, months, weekdays etc. are not captured if they are specified in a custom format passed in `date_formats` unless they are written in English, independently from the languages you pass under `languages`. I add an example below.

```python3
dateparser.parse('xxxx junio', languages=['es'], date_formats=['xxxx %B'])
# >> None
dateparser.parse('xxxx june', languages=['es'], date_formats=['xxxx %B'])
# >> datetime.datetime(2018, 6, 30, 0, 0)
```

issue_title: Support non-printable/emoji characters in comments
issue_body:
Following the merging of #368, non-printable characters encountered by js-yaml seem to throw an error.

I suspect that this is a breaking change that will affect a number of people who happen to have e.g. emoji in comments when they upgrade to 3.10.

Though ignoring non-printables in comments would appears to be a breach of spec, I feel that they should be acceptable (i.e. ignored), as this fix would not substantively affect the parsing or output.  I noted that e.g. the Python yaml parser ignores emoji in comments.

issue_title: `fluent_pages.0001_initial` migration makes wrong assumption about `SITE_ID`.
issue_body:
It assumes that if any `Site` objects exist, that a site matching `settings.SITE_ID` will exist. That's not always true. We have two projects which share the same database, and they have different values for `settings.SITE_ID`.

When I migrate the first project, all good. When I migrate the second project, there is a `Site` object, so this migration doesn't bother creating a new one to match `settings.SITE_ID`, and then I get `django.contrib.sites.models.DoesNotExist: Site matching query does not exist.`

I think it could be fixed by changing the code like this:

```
def make_site(apps, schema_editor):
    Site = apps.get_model("sites", "Site")
    Site.objects.get_or_create(
        pk=settings.SITE_ID,
        defaults=dict(
            name='example',
            domain='example.com',
        ))
```

This also applies to https://github.com/edoburu/django-fluent-contents/issues/55

Any objections?

issue_title: Python3 topic and group names must be bytes objects
issue_body:
In Python3, string startswith does not support bytes object arguments and bytes startswith does not support string object arguments.

Because of this, topic name strings given as a key to client.topics result in a traceback:

```
File "... /python3.4/site-packages/pykafka/cluster.py", line 141, in _should_exclude_topic
    return topic_name.startswith(b"__")
TypeError: startswith first arg must be str or a tuple of str, not bytes
```

After passing the topic name as a bytes object instead, that issue stopped. Then other strangeness happened when attempting to get a balanced consumer from a topic with a specified consumer_group. When the consumer group was specified as a string object keyword argument to get_balanced_consumer, a NoPartitionsForConsumerException occurred. This was fixed by passing the consumer group as a bytes object as well.

issue_title: RFC elasticsearch: allow specifying of unicode character folding
issue_body:
While BibIndex is automatically folding unicode characters using the suboptimal `utf8_general_ci` collation (and in a parallel master-related future PR I am going to propose to allow to configure in a simple way the `utf8_unicode_520_ci` collation for certain indexes), it would be great to take this into account into the early elasticsearch integration into Invenio. In particular it should be easy (and we should have good defaults out of the box) to set the collation/character folding in elasticsearch so that indexes are properly configured.

See: http://www.elasticsearch.org/guide/en/elasticsearch/guide/current/unicode-normalization.html

issue_title: RFC: new bibcirculation module
issue_body:
# Problem
The bibcirculation module might need an overhaul.

# Proposal
As discussed with the library team, the bibcirculation module should be rewritten from scratch, so the purpose of this RFC is to discuss current development stages.

## Current stage: Data structure
It's about what to store in a database.

Two ideas:
```
    1+-----------------+                                          
+--->|Record           |                                          
|    +-----------------+                                          
|    |Won't change this|                                          
|    +-----------------+                                          
|                                                                 
|            +---------------------------------+                  
|            v1                                |                  
|    +--------------+        n+------------+   |                  
|    |ItemManager   |    +--->|Item        |   |                  
|    +--------------+    |    +------------+   |                  
+----+record        |    |    |item_manager+---+                  
     |items: []     +----+ +--+infos       |                      
     |requesters: []+--+   |  |status: []  |                      
     +--------------+  |   |  |history: [] +---------------+      
                       |   |  +------------+               |      
+----------------------+   |           ^n                  |      
|                          |           |                   vn     
|   n+-----------+1        |           |             +-----------+
+---^+Borrower   +^--------+           |             |Event      |
     +-----------+                     |             +-----------+
     |           |                     |             |date       |
     |loans: []  +---------------------+             |description|
     |history: []+---------------------------------->|borrower   |
     ------------+                                  n|item       |
                                                     +-----------+
```

```
    1+-----------------+                       
+--->|Record           |                       
|    +-----------------+                       
|    |Won't change this|                       
|    +-----------------+                       
|                                              
|           +---------------------------------+
|           vn                                |
|    +--------------+        1+-----------+   |
|    |Item          |    +--->|Borrower   |   |
|    +--------------+    |    +-----------+   |
+----+record        |    |    |loans: []  +---+
     |borrower      +----+    |history: []+---+
     |status: []    |         +-----------+   |
+----+history: []   |               ^n        |
|    |requesters: []+---------------+         |
|    +--------------+                         |
|                                             |
|                                             |
|   n+-----------+n                           |
+--->|Event      |<---------------------------+
     +-----------+                             
     |date       |                             
     |description|                             
     |borrower   |                             
     |item       |                             
     +-----------+                             
```

* I know calling any class '*Manager' is a bad idea, but well ^^
* Both outlines should be able to handle the current use cases of
  * Simple loans
  * Simple loans, with unavailable item(s)
  * inter library loan
  * purchase request
* ItemManager?
  * The ItemManager would just handle the steps required for items which are not immediately available
  * The same functionality could be provided using some more elaborate (maybe not?) database queries
* The Event objects are proposed to keep track of loans and item statuses in order to satisfy the need to generate meaningful statistics
* The attributes are dummies

Personally, I suggest the second approach.

Criticism and ideas are highly encouraged :)

issue_title: sorter: default factoty not working for unicode queries
issue_body:
This line https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/blob/master/invenio_records_rest/sorter.py#L134 will make unicode queries return the default `noquery` sorting mechanism.

```python
# Default sort with query
with app.test_request_context("/?q=test"):
    query, urlargs = default_sorter_factory(Search(), 'myindex')
    assert query.to_dict()['sort'] == \
        [{'field1': {'order': 'desc'}}, {'field2': {'order': 'asc'}}]
    assert urlargs == dict(sort='-myfield')

with app.test_request_context("/?q=Oh là là"):
    query, urlargs = default_sorter_factory(Search(), 'myindex')
    assert query.to_dict()['sort'] == \
        [{'field1': {'order': 'desc'}}, {'field2': {'order': 'asc'}}]
    assert urlargs == dict(sort='-myfield')
``` 

Simply changing the line mentioned above by something like:

```
ipdb> request.values.get('q', type=str)
ipdb> request.values.get('q', type=six.text_type)
u'Oh l\xe0 l\xe0'
```

---

Related with https://github.com/zenodo/zenodo/issues/1227

Note 1: maybe it's worth taking a look at all the castings we are doing to `str`, specially if `q` is involved

Note 2: we only use `six` in a couple places, `urllib.parse` and `string_types`, maybe it's worth extending a bit the [_compact.py](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/blob/master/invenio_records_rest/_compat.py) in case we want to remove this dependency.

issue_title: Anonymous Users always get a session cookie
issue_body:
Grok/Zope are great for building websites, but their performance is a bit slow.  In particular most of my users are anonymous users.  I would love to cache the web pages in NGINX, and only generate them occasionally. 

Unfortunately Zope generates a cookie if the request does not have one.  I found the culprit.  It is in zope.pluggableauth.plugins.session.

First it calls ISession(request), then it tries to authenticate.  The ISession() creates a cookie if there is not one, and that kills caching.  It should be reasonably easy to test if their is already a  session cookie, or a login/password combination, and if not, just return and do not create the session cookie. 

I would think that this would be an issue for a lot of people.  I thought Plone was using Zope 3 and pluggable Auth.  Sure they need cookies for langugag, but probably not for every anonymous user.

Am I missing something?

Warm Regards
Chris

issue_title: Multi-packet heap with stop control will lose the data in that heap
issue_body:
Currently, as soon as a packet with a stop control flag is seen, everything gets shut down. However, nothing in the protocol prevents the stop heap from having lots of data spread over multiple heaps (even though this is not how it is typically used for KAT). In such cases, we could wait until the heap is complete before shutting everything down.

This might be WONTFIXed, because making that change increases the risk that other packets in that heap could get lost, leading to a deadlock where the consumer never gets to see the stop packet because it is in a partial heap. A possible workaround is to add a timeout to ensure that everything gets shut down within a short interval of seeing a stop packet.

It is probably best if stop packets contain nothing but the stop control, however.

issue_title: evaluate() generates an extra reference for objects in calling frame
issue_body:
I find that `numexpr.evaluate()` generates an extra ref count for objects in calling frame, even when the object isn't used in the expression.  Here's an example:

```python
import numexpr
import numpy as np
import sys

def f():
    a = np.arange(10)
    numexpr.evaluate('1')
    print(sys.getrefcount(a))

def g():
    a = np.arange(10)
    print(sys.getrefcount(a))

f()
g()
```
```
3
2
```

This created a memory leak in my code: in my application, `a` was a big array that I was deleting, but it was never garbage collected because of the reference created by numexpr in a totally unrelated operation.

Notably, passing `local_dict={}` fixes the problem.  I think the usage of `sys._getframe().f_locals` is creating the persistent reference somehow, perhaps via a caching mechanism.  So this code also exhibits the leak:

```python
def h():
    a = np.arange(10)
    sys._getframe(0).f_locals
    print(sys.getrefcount(a))
h()
```

If I had to guess, I'd say this has to do with CPython fast locals (https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/master/Objects/frameobject.c), but I'm not enough of an expert to be able to tell for certain.

If one captures the f_locals dict then calls `clear()` on it, the leak disappears.  I have no idea if this is safe, though!

issue_title: PyQt GPL "infecting" even when using PySide backend
issue_body:
There happened a useful [conversation on Reddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/Python/comments/8rpg23/pyappshare_cookbook_for_creating_crossplatform/) about publishing python apps that depend on PySide and MIT/BSD wrappers around PySide/PyQt like qtpy/pyqtgraph/matplotlib. [Backup of the discussion](https://github.com/kiwi0fruit/pyappshare/files/2110002/pyappshare_reddit.zip).

According that conversation there are concerns that using qtpy/pyqtgraph/matplotlib in the propriate app even with PySide backend may lead to GPL violation.  The problems came from two points:

* even trying to import from PyQt may lead to the GPL "infecting",
* even having PyQt as a dependency may lead to the GPL "infecting" (so that pip/conda installation would install pyqt automatically).

After all the discussion on Reddit the best way to solve the problem seems to do that upstream: make sure that there is a reliable switch in the qtpy/pyqtgraph/matplotlib modules that effectively and reliably turns the app that uses them (app as a whole) to something like:

```py
if False:
    import PyQt
```

Maybe such switch is already there - but this needs checking. It's also would be great to unify that interface among modules.

And also remove PyQt from their dependencies so that they do not auto-download PyQt (I guess most of them already behave this way).

issue_title: just importing qtpy should not have side effects
issue_body:
Hi, qtpy is breaking code that does not even use it. This was already warned by @titusjan in https://github.com/spyder-ide/qtpy/pull/65#issuecomment-252472704

The following is a snippet where the problem is demonstrated. A PyQt4 program that does not use qtpy but imports it (perhaps it imports some other module that imports qtpy) gets broken. If the qtpy import is removed, the program reaches the `print 3` line, but if the import is done, an exception prevents reaching that line.

```python
import sip
sip.setapi('QString', 2)
sip.setapi('QVariant', 2)
sip.setapi('QDate', 2)
sip.setapi('QDateTime', 2)
sip.setapi('QTextStream', 2)
sip.setapi('QTime', 2)
sip.setapi('QUrl', 2)

from PyQt4 import QtGui, QtCore
app = QtGui.QApplication([])

w = QtGui.QHeaderView(QtCore.Qt.Vertical)
w.setResizeMode(w.Fixed)
print 1, w.setResizeMode

# Importing qtpy.Qwidgets changes w!!! (even if never using qtpy)
import qtpy.QtWidgets

print 2, w.setResizeMode
w.setResizeMode(w.Fixed)
print 3
```

IMHO, simply importing qtpy should never affect programs that do not use it (in the case of https://github.com/taurus-org/taurus/issues/401 , just importing `spyder.widgets.editor` breaks our module ). 

Would it be possible to avoid patching those methods? (or at least doing something less disruptive than raising an exception?)

Cheers!

issue_title: BUG: Roll() function is incorrect
issue_body:
It appears that the roll() function in the utils package is incorrect. There are two problems:

For the examples below we will assume the pd.series has 10 data points and the window argument is 5.

1. The function does not calculate for the last period. The source of this problem is the definition of the range in the for loop. As it is now:

`for I in range(window, len(args[0])):`

The loop will run for i values of 5 to 9 because the len of the series is 10, but the range() function is non-inclusive of the last number. So the next line that extracts the subset of the pd.series or np.array to run the function over:

numpy array:  `rets = [s[i - window:i] for s in args]`
pandas series: `rets = [s.iloc[i - window:i] for s in args]`

on the last pass of the for loop i=9 and thus the last data point in the series is never included in the calculation.

2. The next issue is that for pandas series the above causes the results to be mapped to the wrong index. When the i in the for loop is 9, the maximum number of the loop, and thus the function is applied to elements [4, 9] the result is assigned the datetime index of 9, which is incorrect because that is the last value in the index and does not properly align to the data used in the function which is .iloc[4, 9]

How to see all of this:

For a given raw series below and using the roll() function of np.nansum() is a column of what we would expect, and what is actually returned.
```
	          Input Series	Expected	               Actual
1/1/01	                   1 		
1/2/01	                   2 		
1/3/01	                   3 		
1/4/01	                   4 		
1/5/01	                   5 	            15 	
1/6/01	                   6 	            20 	            15 
1/7/01	                   7 	            25 	            20 
1/8/01	                   8 	            30 	            25 
1/9/01	                   9 	            35 	            30 
1/10/01	                10 	            40 	            35 
```

**What is the fix?** 
It is simple in the _roll_ndarray() and _roll_pandas() functions make the range end at len() + 1:

`for i in range(window, len(args[0]) + 1):`

And for the _roll_pandas() then for the datetime index `i - 1`

`data[args[0]. index[i - 1]] = func(*rets, **kwargs)`

**Why didn't any test catch this?**

It appears that the test for the roll() function, test_pandas_roll() was incorrect. It expects the length of the result series to be the length of the input series minus the window. That is incorrect, the expected number of return elements is that PLUS one.

**What is the solution?**

Simple to fix the two offending functions in utils.py:
```
def _roll_ndarray(func, window, *args, **kwargs):
    data = []
    for i in range(window, len(args[0]) + 1):
        rets = [s[i-window:i] for s in args]
        data.append(func(*rets, **kwargs))
    return np.array(data)
```

```
def _roll_pandas(func, window, *args, **kwargs):
    data = {}
    for i in range(window, len(args[0]) + 1):
        rets = [s.iloc[i-window:i] for s in args]
        data[args[0].index[i - 1]] = func(*rets, **kwargs)
    return pd.Series(data)
```
**Why not do this?**

This change will cause 52 tests that were incorrectly passing to now break. I am happy to submit the PR, but I do not have the time now to correct all the broken tests.

If the package maintainers would like I can submit this PR and then over time if people want to help we can fix the tests. I don't have time now to fix them all myself.

issue_title: TypeError: _on_request() takes exactly 3 arguments (8 given)
issue_body:
I use `responses` while testing a few calls to a REST API. The REST API uses `requests.HTTPAdapter` in one form or the other. Requests are sent using `HTTPAdapter.send()` (https://github.com/kennethreitz/requests/blob/master/requests/adapters.py#L375)

Mocking this request with `responses` fails (at least when I have not prepared a response) with the following error message:

```
adapter = <requests.HTTPAdapter object at 0x105a8b890>
request = <PreparedRequest [GET]>, a = (False, None, True, None, None)
kwargs = {}

    def unbound_on_send(adapter, request, *a, **kwargs):
>       return self._on_request(adapter, request, *a, **kwargs)
E       TypeError: _on_request() takes exactly 3 arguments (8 given)
```
`unbound_on_send` is located in `responses`. The values for parameter `a` are the (default) parameters passed to `HTTPAdapter.send()`.

These are the relevant lines in `responses`:
* https://github.com/getsentry/responses/blob/master/responses.py#L297 (call with expanded arguments)
* https://github.com/getsentry/responses/blob/master/responses.py#L231 (callee does not support extra positional arguments)

issue_title: util.from_n3() is broken wrt. Literals with datatypes
issue_body:
```python
In [1]: import rdflib
INFO:rdflib:RDFLib Version: 4.2.1-dev

In [2]: rdflib.util.from_n3('"42"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer>')
WARNING:rdflib.term:<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer> does not look like a valid URI, trying to serialize this will break.
Out[2]: rdflib.term.Literal(u'42', datatype=rdflib.term.URIRef(u'<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer>'))
```
problem here is that the `<` is passed into `URIRef()` as part of the URI.

```python
In [3]: rdflib.util.from_n3('"42"^^xsd:integer')
Out[3]: rdflib.term.Literal(u'42', datatype=rdflib.term.URIRef(u'xsd:integer'))
```
problem is that standard xsd datatypes aren't resolved correctly but instead passed into `URIRef()` as is.

```python
In [4]: rdflib.util.from_n3('"42"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer')
Out[4]: rdflib.term.Literal(u'42', datatype=rdflib.term.URIRef(u'http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer'))

In [5]: rdflib.Literal(42) == rdflib.util.from_n3('"42"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer')
Out[5]: True
```
this is actually invalid n3 but the only thing that returns the expected `Literal`

More fun (round-tripping):
```python
In [6]: rdflib.util.from_n3(rdflib.Literal(42).n3()).n3()
WARNING:rdflib.term:<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer> does not look like a valid URI, trying to serialize this will break.
Out[6]: u'"42"^^<<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer>>'
```

issue_title: Bad relative URI resolution in SPARQL
issue_body:
When using an explicit base URI in SPARQL queries, relative URIs are not correctly resolved.
Instead, the relative URI is simply appended at the end of the base URI.

Example:
```python
import rdflib

g = rdflib.Graph()
r = g.query("SELECT (<../baz> as ?test) WHERE {}",
            base=rdflib.URIRef("http://example.org/foo/bar"))
print r.serialize(format="csv")

# expected result:
#test
#http://example.org/baz

# actual result;
#test
#http://example.org/foo/bar../baz
```

issue_title: Implement versioned serialization read format for 3.8
allow numpy calls in functions in configs
issue_body:
Allow either `numpy.` or `np.` calls, for example the following.  I know this works in other scenarios such as Eval so should be straightforward to implement

```
        type: GBeta
        g:
            # this didn't work, due to np.exp
            type: RandomDistribution
            function: "(1-x**2)**2 * np.exp( -0.5 * x**2 / 0.2**2 )"
            x_min: 0.0
            x_max: 1.0
        beta:
            type: Random

```

issue_title: 1.2 release not deployed on pypi
issue_body: The version number in the last release has not been updated to `1.2`, so the deployment of the release has been made with the wrong version (namely [`1.1.2.dev0`](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/neurom/1.1.2.dev0). I think this could be fixed with a correctly versioned `1.2.1` release.

issue_title: Make neurom.fst._neuritefunc.iter_sections functionality public
issue_body: This allows convenient iteration over all the sections of a neurite, neuron or population, allowing the user to specify the type of iteration (either the order, or via filtering iterators such as `ileaf` and `ibifurcation`.) It is currently private but could be made accessible, like `neurom.iter_neurites`.

issue_title: After upgrade: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property 'library' of undefined
issue_body:
After upgrading my webpack packages to:
```
    "@fortawesome/fontawesome": "^1.2.0-6",
    "@fortawesome/fontawesome-free-brands": "^5.0.13",
    "@fortawesome/fontawesome-free-regular": "^5.1.0-3",
    "@fortawesome/fontawesome-free-solid": "^5.1.0-3",
    "@fortawesome/fontawesome-svg-core": "^1.2.0-11",
    "@fortawesome/vue-fontawesome": "^0.1.0-4",
```

And following your guide about adding icons to a fontawesome library via a special icons.js file:
```
import fontawesome from '@fortawesome/fontawesome'
import brands from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-free-brands'
import { faSpinner } from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-free-solid'

fontawesome.library.add(brands, faSpinner)
```

I import above icons.js in my boot.js which also contains the Vuejs main app.


I get the following error when starting the brower (Chrome): 

> Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property 'library' of undefined

Am I doing something wrong or is something broken in the newest version?

Kind regards,
Henry

issue_title: Ability to disable query-string versioning
issue_body:
When using an asset fingerprinting solution like [broccoli-asset-rev](https://github.com/rickharrison/broccoli-asset-rev):
- File types are detected by the file extension, which essentially will be something like `.svg?v=0.4.2` - something these libs won't recoginze.
- These libraries make query-string versioning unnecessary, since assets are served with an MD5 hash in the filname.

A variable like `$fa-query-string-version: true`/`@fa-query-string-version: true` would be a good addition to have for Font Awesome to be a better citizen in these (not that exotic) environments.

issue_title: Postgres timeline needlessly advanced during startup of primary node
issue_body:
It appears Patroni considers every Postgres shutdown a fatal crash. 

In the event an existing cluster is started following a controlled shutdown, Patroni always creates a `recovery.conf` file, even when no recovery is necessary. This advances the internal instance timeline, as Postgres assumes an uncontrolled shutdown and executes the full recovery stack.

To prevent this, Patroni should:

1. Obtain the primary lock, if possible.
2. Lock obtained, attempt to start Postgres without recovery mechanisms.
3. If Postgres starts normally, no further action is taken.
4. If Postgres will not start or reports an error necessitating recovery, create a `recovery.conf` file and follow the current protocol.

issue_title: s4cmd 2.0.1 leaks memory for large uploads
issue_body:
Hi there:

We are using s4cmd to push, in parallel, about 70k files into an S3 bucket.  The files vary in size from very small (10s of bytes) to somewhat large (11GB).  On a recent run, s4cmd consumed nearly 80GB of memory before being caught by the Linux OOM killer.

To test, I created a smaller pool of 2,500 and 1,000 files and modified s4cmd to sleep before exiting so we could get an accurate RSS measurement.

The 2,500 file pool was 6.9GB on disk.  The 1,000 file pool was 2.5GB on disk.

After successfully uploading both pools, RSS for the s4cmd process was roughly equal to the total size of files uploaded:

The 2.5GB pool ended up with an RSS of 2.7GB.

The 6.1GB pool ended up with an RSS of 7.3GB.

The size of the files being uploaded, not the total number of files, is the driving factor.  Uploading 5,000 2 byte files only drives the RSS to .16GB (~167MB).

Specifying a very small max-singlepart-upload-size (for example, 1048576 bytes) does not change the memory usage.

After bisecting the upload() method in s4cmd.py, it appears the leak or cycle is happening inside of the boto3 put_object() call.  Commenting out the put_object() keeps memory usage stable.

Importing and periodically calling gc.collect() also keeps memory usage in check.

I have not put together a test case just using boto3 to see if the leak occurs there.  But I can't be the only person who would like to use s4cmd to upload several hundreds of GB of files.

Is there already a known workaround for this problem?

The environment is:

python 2.7.12

Add a similar feature to before_request as is provided in Flask: http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/0.12/api/#flask.Flask.before_request

This allows a function to be run before the actual route function is called. It is very useful for doing request parameter validation, user identification and permission verification etc.

issue_title: HTTP status code 200 hardcoded in generated Swagger
issue_body:
I have an API Gateway method that returns a 202. Chalice allows for custom responses, so cool. However, the custom HTTP response code is ignored in the API Gateway auto-generated Swagger etc.  

#### Example

```python
return Response(body=json.loads(foo), 
    status_code=202, 
    headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'})
```

The generated swagger (from the API Gateway export) has the following instead of the 202 I want there (and yes, it matters).

```json
"responses": {
          "200": {
            "description": "200 response",
            "schema": {
              "$ref": "#/definitions/Empty"
            }
          }
        }
```

Looking at `_generate_precanned_responses` in [`swagger.py`](https://github.com/aws/chalice/blob/3bfbd3d6800777206198fc58d264eaee1c4f574d/chalice/deploy/swagger.py#L145) I see it's hardcoded. There are other places as well (`_add_preflight_request`, `_generate_apig_integ`).

It looks like the `Response` object is not accessible during the package/deploy phase (please let me know if I missed something). Given the custom response is specific to a route/verb combo (it is in my case but that shouldn't matter), I experimented with adding a parameter to the `@app.route`.

```python
@app.route(API_BASE_PATH + '/tasks', 
    methods=['POST'], 
    content_types=['application/json'], 
    custom_response_code=202)
```

I have a solution that works for me and if this is useful to the community, I can submit a PR. 

I'm really interested in your thoughts on this and how you might go about dealing with it.

Thank you.

issue_title: TensorTools Randomize Functions could be CUDAfied
issue_body: Currently generating random numbers is done on the CPU and these random numbers are transferred to the GPU. It would be nice if we could generate random numbers directly on the GPU as it would presumably be much faster. (The GPU random seed would also need to be set with dynet-seed to ensure consistency across runs.)

issue_title: Retry temporary failures for Bigtable scans
issue_body: Cloud Bigtable can return DEADLINE_EXCEEDED after 5 minutes on a scan request, even if there are more responses to be returned.   In that case, or INTERNAL, UNAVAILABLE or ABORTED, the scan should be retried from the key after the one previously returned in the scan.

issue_title: Pub/sub: amount of streaming pull operations way higher than streaming ack operations
issue_labels: [{'id': 139685153, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20pubsub', 'name': 'api: pubsub', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 778245707, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/status:%20awaiting%20information', 'name': 'status: awaiting information', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 810248285, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/triaged%20for%20GA', 'name': 'triaged for GA', 'color': 'b5a1ea', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438584, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20question', 'name': 'type: question', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
We are using `python 2.7.12` with `google-cloud-pubsub==0.30.1` and `grpcio==1.9.1` and have a very basic subscriber script:

    import random
    import time

    from google.cloud import pubsub

    GCLOUD_PROJECT_ID = <our_project_id>
    SUBSCRIPTION_NAME = <subscription_name>

    def pubsub_message_callback(message):
        time.sleep(random.random())
        message.ack()

    def main():
        sub_client = pubsub.SubscriberClient()
        full_subscription_name = 'projects/{project_id}/subscriptions/{subscription}'.format(
            project_id=GCLOUD_PROJECT_ID,
            subscription=SUBSCRIPTION_NAME,
        )
        pubsub_subscription = sub_client.subscribe(
            full_subscription_name,
            flow_control=pubsub.types.FlowControl(max_messages=1)
        )
        pubsub_subscription.open(
            pubsub_message_callback
        )
        while True:
            pass

    if __name__ == '__main__':
        main()

When having a large backlog and running this script for 20 minutes, the amount of streaming pull operations is 100 times the amount of streaming ack operations. [(see image)](https://i.stack.imgur.com/rFa7l.png)

As the the flow control is set to `max_messages=1` I would expect that it will always wait to ack the received message, before receiving another one. And even when flow control was not set to `max_messages=1` it still looks like unintended behaviour as the total amount of pull operations is still way higher than the amount of ack operations (so what happens with those received/unacked messages?). Is this a bug or is it intended behaviour?

Also asked this as a question on [stackoverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/48640525/gcp-pub-sub-python-client-amount-of-streaming-pull-messages-way-higher-than-str)

issue_title: GCS Blob does not support the io.IOBase interface
issue_body:
I'm branching this issue from the (currently resolved) issue #2871. The title covers the request, but to add some context see below.

Many python libraries (pandas, biopython, pillow, etc.) support reading from a 'python file object' which canonically are objects that implement the [io.IOBase](https://docs.python.org/2/library/io.html#io.IOBase) interface. If we could open a blob as a file object, we could do operations as shown in the example below where I'm just reading a few lines from a (possibly very large) file. While the example could be recreated by partially downloading into a 

```
from Bio import SeqIO
from google.cloud.storage import blob

with blob.Blob("/data/example.fastq", "mybucket").open("rb") as fileobj:
    for i, rec in enumerate(SeqIO.parse(fileobj, "fastq")):
        print(rec.seq)
        print('  name=%s\n  annotations=%r' % (rec.name, rec.annotations))
        if i > 5:
            break
```
[`google-resumable-media`](https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-resumable-media-python/latest/) covers some of the need expressed in this bug, but it does not satisfy users who need blobs to be parsed by libraries expecting standard file objects. The standard advice might be to download files first but that advice ignores the expense and time of downloading large files when indexed operations are available.

Some work has been done towards this end - the following two repositories have solutions. That said, I think most people would be far more happy to adopt a solution from this project since it carries the implicit Google endorsement. 
- https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues
- http://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/google-cloud-python

issue_title: `Blob.upload_from_filename` fails silently if the blob name starts with '/'
issue_labels: [{'id': 77678602, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20storage', 'name': 'api: storage', 'color': 'd4af37', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2+', 'name': 'priority: p2+', 'color': 'ffee58', 'default': False}, {'id': 663697852, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/status:%20investigating', 'name': 'status: investigating', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Using `google-cloud-storage==1.2.0`, attempting to upload files using `Blob.upload_from_filename` fails silently (i.e. no error is raised but the blob is not uploaded) if the blob name starts with a forward slash `/`. A basic example is given below.

```python
import google.cloud.storage

client = google.cloud.storage.Client()
bucket = client.get_bucket('some-bucket')
blob = bucket("/some-blob")
blob.upload_from_filename("some-file")
```

issue_title: page_token parameter documentation is misleading
issue_labels: [{'id': 82126549, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20bigquery', 'name': 'api: bigquery', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 77676134, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/documentation', 'name': 'documentation', 'color': 'bfdadc', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2', 'name': 'priority: p2', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 744431537, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20process', 'name': 'type: process', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
The `page_token` parameter to several api functions such as `list_projects` and `list_jobs` is documented as follows:
> opaque marker for the next "page" of projects. If not passed, the API will return the first page of projects.

This is very misleading, as these functions return an `HTTPIterator` which if iterated on will seamlessly return multiple pages of results.
Only though experimentation did I determine that multiple pages of results are returned and that if I did want to make use of `page_token` I'd need to access it at the `next_page_token` of the  `HTTPIterator`.

I think the documentation should be updated to better reflect that multiple pages can be fetched without touching the `page_token` and possibly include where to access `page_token` if someone does need to use it.

issue_title: subtle bug in gcimagebuilder/utils.py xfs detection
issue_body:
Currently, line 97: `if self.type is 'xfs':`
should read `if self.type == 'xfs':`
and line 192: `if fs_type is 'xfs':`
should read: `if fs_type == 'xfs':`

The `is` operator explicitly compares object _identity_ rather than _value_ equality. Hat tip to @sjagoe for bug solution.

issue_title: Update Wiki Setup-a-Shadowsocks-relay
issue_body:
Link: [https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks/wiki/Setup-a-Shadowsocks-relay](https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks/wiki/Setup-a-Shadowsocks-relay)

With Redhat/Fedora/CentOS have used the new firewall service, it's easier to relay the shadowsocks services.
See also: https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/Fedora/19/html/Security_Guide/sec-Configure_Port_Forwarding-CLI.html

```bash
firewall-cmd --add-masquerade --permanent
firewall-cmd --add-forward-port=port=2222:proto=tcp:toport=1234:toaddr=1.2.3.4 --permanent
firewall-cmd --add-forward-port=port=2222:proto=udp:toport=1234:toaddr=1.2.3.4 --permanent
```

It's also shows on my blog, [http://blog.vrqq.org/archives/23/](http://blog.vrqq.org/archives/23/ ).

issue_title: 如何避免服务器下载的文件所属用户被修改？
issue_body:
例如，在服务器上使用shadowsocks用户运行ss-manager：
```sh
# ps axu | grep ss-
shadows+ 28358  0.0  0.1  23324   296 ?        Ss   6月25   0:00 /usr/bin/ss-manager -c /etc/shadowsocks-libev/manager.json -u
shadows+ 28361  0.0  1.0  25196  2820 ?        Ss   6月25   0:16 ss-server --manager-address 127.0.0.1:8839 -f /var/lib/shadowsocks/.shadowsocks/.shadowsocks_44444.pid -c /var/lib/shadowsocks/.shadowsocks/.shadowsocks_44444.conf -t 60 -u -s 0.0.0.0
shadows+ 28364  0.0  0.9  25028  2464 ?        Ss   6月25   0:19 ss-server --manager-address 127.0.0.1:8839 -f /var/lib/shadowsocks/.shadowsocks/.shadowsocks_44445.pid -c /var/lib/shadowsocks/.shadowsocks/.shadowsocks_44445.conf -t 60 -u -s 0.0.0.0
```
以root用户登录服务器后，使用wget下载一个tar包，tar包是root用户的，但解压后的文件所属用户都是shadowsocks：
```sh
# ls -l
drwxr-xr-x 11 shadowsocks shadowsocks    4096 3月  22 2014 mldonkey-3.1.5
-rw-r--r--  1 root        root        2847382 3月  22 2014 mldonkey-3.1.5.tar.bz2
```

```

        Tags=[
            Tag("Environment", "Production", True),
            Tag('Name', name, True),
            Tag('Project', project, True),
            Tag('GitTag', tag_git, True),
            Tag('Stack', 'v_{}'.format(build_version), True)

            ),

            )
        )
    )

issue_title: `pants server` entries don't reflect the proper cli invocation when executed through pantsd.
"Unnormalized" addresses not validated in BUILD files
issue_body:
The error for unnormalized addresses in BUILD files seems to have broken at some point, so people's repos have likely begun to backslide in this area.

In particular, the following addresses used to be legal on the command line but banned in BUILD files, but are currently legal:
```
src/lang/some/target:target
/src/lang/some/target
```
etc.

issue_title: Junit runner exits JVM with error 4 under experimental runner, no other messages
issue_body:
One of our teams ran into a long running test that at the end fails with:

```
FAILURE:
java org.pantsbuild.tools.junit.ConsoleRunner ... exited non-zero (4); 0 failed 0 targets.
```

When I was asked to debug it I remarked, "Its as if something called `System.exit(4)`"

Fortunately, we still have maven setup in our repo for one more week and found:

```
[ERROR] Failed to execute goal org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-failsafe-plugin:2.19:integration-test (integration-test) on project acceptance-tests: Execution integration-test of goal org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-failsafe-plugin:2.19:integration-test failed: java.lang.RuntimeException: org.apache.maven.surefire.report.ReporterException: When writing report: /payjenkinsbuild/build-archives/regis-fullstack/workspace/payments/acceptance-tests/target/failsafe-reports/TEST-com.squareup.payments.acceptancetests.regis.RegisTenderAcceptanceIT[AddTenderAmountParameter.NORMAL_VALUE, TimeZoneParameter.NONE, MerchantParameter.JP_MERCHANT_AUTHORIZED_FOR_JCB, MediaTypeParameter.URL_ENCODED, CardTenderParameter.KEYED_EMPTY_AVS].xml (File name too long) -> [Help 1]
[ERROR] 
```

This is where the `System.exit(4)` is coming from:

```
src/java/org/pantsbuild/tools/junit/impl/ConsoleRunnerImpl.java
451:        // This error might be a call to `System.exit(0)`, which we definitely do
832:      System.exit(code);
```

And some information about the error:

Linux has a maximum filename length of 255 characters for most filesystems (including EXT4), and a maximum path of 4096 characters. eCryptfs is a layered filesystem. It stacks on top of another filesystem such as EXT4, which is actually used to write data to the disk. Feb 27, 2012

issue_title: test-changed doesn't appear to respect exclude_target_regexp
issue_body:
I am testing 1.2.0rc1 in our repo and passing `exclude_target_regexp`  to `test-changed` doesn't appear to be working.

I was able to hack it working with:
```
--- a/src/python/pants/task/changed_target_task.py
+++ b/src/python/pants/task/changed_target_task.py
@@ -49,7 +49,8 @@ class ChangedTargetTask(NoopExecTask):
   def alternate_target_roots(cls, options, address_mapper, build_graph):
     changed = Changed.Factory.global_instance().create(options)
     change_calculator = changed.change_calculator(build_graph=build_graph,
-                                                  address_mapper=address_mapper)
+                                                  address_mapper=address_mapper,
+                                                  exclude_target_regexp=options.exclude_target_regexp)
     changed_addresses = change_calculator.changed_target_addresses()
     readable = ''.join(sorted('\n\t* {}'.format(addr.reference()) for addr in changed_addresses))
     logger.info('Operating on changed {} target(s): {}'.format(len(changed_addresses), readable))
```

The rest of the options look to be gathered from the ChangedRequest.from_options(chained_options), it is possible the "correct" thing is to change the excludes to be also attached the ChangedRequest.


Here is a repro from Pants 1.2.0rc1 checkout:
```
mateo:pants mateo$ git st
HEAD detached at release_1.2.0rc1
	modified:   src/python/pants/core_tasks/what_changed.py
```

**Before the above patch**:
```
mateo:pants mateo$ ./pants test-changed
04:55:20 00:00     [parse]INFO] Operating on changed 1 target(s):
	* src/python/pants/core_tasks

mateo:pants mateo$ ./pants test-changed --test-changed-exclude-target-regexp="['src']"
04:55:35 00:00     [parse]INFO] Operating on changed 1 target(s):
	* src/python/pants/core_tasks
```
**After the above patch was applied**:
```
mateo:pants mateo$ ./pants test-changed
04:57:47 00:00     [parse]INFO] Operating on changed 1 target(s):
	* src/python/pants/core_tasks

mateo:pants mateo$ ./pants test-changed --test-changed-exclude-target-regexp="['src']"
04:58:46 00:00     [parse]INFO] Operating on changed 0 target(s):
```

issue_title: validate export and/or serving and better error messaging for onnx-exported models
issue_body:
The `signature.json` file describes the `data_name`, and if you put in some arbitrary value, you can get an export from an onnx model. However, the serving step will fail because it is looking for `input_0`. This relationship wasn't specified in the docs (as of yet). The user will get the error: ``` (mxnet_p36) 8c8590217d26:onnx markhama$ mxnet-model-server --models o=o.model [INFO 2018-01-15 17:09:57,821 PID:45329 /Users/markhama/Development/mxnet_p36/lib/python2.7/site-packages/mxnet_model_server-0.2-py2.7.egg/mms/mxnet_model_server.py:__init__:94] Initialized model serving. [WARNING 2018-01-15 17:09:57,887 PID:45329 /Users/markhama/Development/mxnet_p36/lib/python2.7/site-packages/mxnet_model_server-0.2-py2.7.egg/mms/model_service/mxnet_model_service.py:__init__:94] Failed to parse epoch from param file, setting epoch to 0 [ERROR 2018-01-15 17:09:57,897 PID:45329 /Users/markhama/Development/mxnet_p36/lib/python2.7/site-packages/mxnet_model_server-0.2-py2.7.egg/mms/mxnet_model_server.py:_arg_process:185] Failed to process arguments: You created Module with Module(..., data_names=[u'onnx']) but input with name 'onnx' is not found in symbol.list_arguments(). Did you mean one of: input_0 param_0 ....more layers... ``` ## Expected behavior I'd expect that if the server was going to fail when I provide anything other than `input_0`, that the exporter would have warned me that my signature was invalid... or just update it for me. Also, since there is a tight relationship on how you POST data, I would like to know that this change is required, otherwise I'd never get an onnx-exported model to serve. This also means that I'd expect the error message coming from a "bad" POST to be more revealing. I don't think we need to obfuscate it. ``` 8c8590217d26:test markhama$ curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8080/squeezenet/predict -F "data=@kitten.jpg" 400 Bad Request

Bad Request

expected bytes-like object, not NoneType

``` Something like: ``` This end point, squeezenet, is expecting a data_name of input_0. End point details can be found here: http://127.0.0.1:8080/squeezenet/api-description ```

issue_title: SAM Ignores Metadata section in template
issue_body: As soon as you declare "Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31" in the template, it no longer respects the Metadata top level section where you can define "AWS::CloudFormation::Interface" to nicely print parameters.   If I comment out the transform line, then it displays my Parameters Groups.  It would be nice if this were respected since it makes the parameters much easier to read and grouped into sections.

Thanks!

issue_title: Breakline instead of newline in tables
issue_body:
I've got two source *.docx files with one table inside each of them. I need to generate a new file with new table containing first columns of source files' tables.
As far as I know, there's no way to simly copy first columns from source tables and assign them to new table. I mean, next code won't work:
```python
col1 = doc1.tables[0].columns[0]
col2 = doc2.tables[0].columns[0]
new_table.columns[0] = col1
new_table.columns[1] = col2
```

issue_title: opinel 1.0x seems to have introduced a 75-second delay.
issue_body:
opinel 1.0x seems to have introduced a (roughly) 75-second delay to any anything making calls to the AWS SDK, whether running one of the `AWS-recipes` Python scripts or running `Scout2`.

For instance, when running `aws_recipes_assume_role.py`, there's a 75-second delay before results are returned and the credentials are cached in the `~/.aws/cli/cache` directory.

When running `aws_recipes_init_sts_session.py`, there's a 75-second delay before it prompts for the MFA code.

When running `aws_recipes_create_ip_ranges.py` it says "Fetching public IP information for the 'xxxxxxx' environment..." and then pauses for 75 seconds before it starts iterating through the various regions and displaying "...in us-east-1: EC2 instances", etc.

After that initial 75-second delay, everything runs as expected. (For instance, there's not an additional 75-second delay for each step of the Scout2 report generation process, only at the very beginning.)

And running the scripts or Scout2 with the `--debug` option returns no additional information. The scripts are succeeding; they just take 75 seconds longer than expected.

issue_title: Bug in the ElementTree append?
issue_body:
Consider the following code:

```
import html5lib
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

example_XML="""<?xml version="1.0"?>
<data>
    <country name="Liechtenstein">
    </country>
 </data>
"""

example_HTML="""<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
    <head>
    </head>
    <body>
    </body>
</html>
"""

tree = html5lib.parse(example_HTML, namespaceHTMLElements=False)
# tree = ET.fromstring(example_XML)
q = ET.Element('rubbish')
tree.append(q)
```

Running the code as is leads to:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/ivan/Desktop/test.py", line 23, in <module>
    tree.append(q)
TypeError: must be Element, not Element
```

Running the code switching off the ``html5lib`` implementation in favor of XML parsing, ie, 

```
# tree = html5lib.parse(example_HTML)
tree = ET.fromstring(example_XML)
```

works. This smells like a bug:-(

issue_title: [Profiling] Humongous CPU with event_stream
issue_body:
I recently developed with the help of your client, which is quite awesome, a listener for marathon events using the event_stream() method.

The idea is simple, listen for the "deployment_step_success" event and execute some internal business code when this happens.

During the development/test phase I used a local marathon installation to provide the events to my code but when the code went live some problems started to happen.

The code seemed much slower than I expected and the CPU usage was ginormous.

After a quick profiling I discovered the problem was lying on the SSECLIENT lib that is used behind the scenes then I extracted the bit of code that actually listens for the events and performed another test.

```python
from marathon import MarathonClient

marathon_client = MarathonClient(marathon_address)

for _event in marathon_client.event_stream():
    print("Got event : {}".format(_event.event_type))
```

**OBS: The mesos/marathon cluster on production is around 60 apps with 200 tasks total**

This simple code hangs about two minutes before outputs "Got event whatever" !

Htop output:
![screenshot from 2016-08-22 12-02-14](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1608212/17859536/62da2920-6860-11e6-97ec-2fd5b0e23735.png)


Here's the cProfiling output:
```
17857859 function calls (16810521 primitive calls) in 425.454 seconds

   Ordered by: cumulative time

   ncalls  tottime  percall  cumtime  percall filename:lineno(function)
    143/1    0.007    0.000  425.454  425.454 {built-in method exec}
        1    0.001    0.001  425.454  425.454 events.py:1(<module>)
       22    0.001    0.000  425.326   19.333 client.py:696(event_stream)
       38    4.582    0.121  425.004   11.184 sseclient.py:58(__next__)
   522457    0.650    0.000  268.910    0.001 sseclient.py:52(_event_complete)
   522458    0.644    0.000  268.261    0.001 re.py:167(search)
   522464  266.073    0.001  266.073    0.001 {method 'search' of '_sre.SRE_Pattern' objects}
1567259/522420    0.770    0.000  151.506    0.000 {built-in method next}
   522420    0.696    0.000  151.198    0.000 utils.py:368(stream_decode_response_unicode)
   522420    0.279    0.000  149.246    0.000 models.py:672(generate)
   522420    0.975    0.000  148.967    0.000 response.py:336(stream)
   522420    3.639    0.000  146.838    0.000 response.py:276(read)
   522420    1.078    0.000  134.903    0.000 client.py:518(read)
   522425    0.731    0.000  133.973    0.000 {method 'read' of '_io._RawIOBase' objects}
   522420    0.656    0.000  133.094    0.000 client.py:549(readinto)
       85    0.000    0.000  132.453    1.558 socket.py:360(readinto)
       85  132.452    1.558  132.452    1.558 {method 'recv_into' of '_socket.socket' objects}

```

Besides "recv_into" which is too low level, the regex is executes half a million times which is responsible for the most time spent during the whole execution.

Any ideas of how to workaround this ?

issue_title: Specifying output file to indexer reports error if path elements are not supplied
issue_body:
```
ipwb index /some/archive.warc -o myindex.cdxj
```
reports `[Errno 2] No such file or directory: ''`.

The remedy is to supply the relative path indicator to the file (`./myindex.cdxj` instead) but in other places, like `ipwb replay myindex.cdxj`, this relative path is not necessary.

We ought to

- [ ] Be consistent with whether we require the path element in indexer and replay
- [ ] Allow files to be specified for output from the indexer with an assumed path (`foo.cdxj` specified implies `./foo.cdxj`)

issue_title: Filters: negated label expressions don't appear to be supported
issue_body:
The [engine docs](https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.34/#operation/NetworkPrune) specify that you can negate label expressions

- `label` (`label=<key>`, `label=<key>=<value>`, `label!=<key>`, or `label!=<key>=<value>`) Prune networks with (or without, in case `label!=`... is used) the specified labels.

Frankly, the engine docs here leave a lot to be desired, there's no example of a negated mapping, so I don't know what format the engine expects. `docker.utils.utils.convert_filters()` just formats the filters as a JSON map of filter name (e.g. `label`) to a list of label expressions. I tried running `client.prune_networks(filters={'label': '!=foo=bar'})` and no networks were deleted, despite me having created two networks, one with `foo=bar` and the other with `foo=baz`.

Am I just going about this wrong, or are negative expressions not supported.

issue_title: Require user to specify API version or use unversioned API endpoint
issue_body:
Hi,

I think the current situation around versioning is less than ideal if you don't provide a API version.
In that case docker-py uses the latest version which is required by all features it uses.

This means that you can't use a docker-py version which supports a feature which was more recently implemented than the oldest docker host you need to connect to. From a ops POV that requires you to pin the docker-py version especially if you depend on 3rd party tools (like ansible) where it's not easy to provide the API version[1]. 

This is really hard to handle and I think there are two ways better than this:

1) Be explicit and always provide a version. This way things can't break and you can always use the most recent docker-py version. This is definitely the preferred way but requires 3rd party tools who use docker-py to provide reasonable ways to set the version.
2) Be lazy and use the unversioned API endpoint. This works for most things and if an API endpoint has changed, the daemon will error out.

From a pragmatic POV I would just use the unversioned API endpoint and leave it to the users whether they want to provide a version. If we want to force people into being explicit (and therefor make sure all tools interacting with docker via docker-py provide easy ways to specify the version), we should require a version.
Anyway, I think just using the latest version by default isn't ideal.

[1]: It's possible to specify the version, but you need to do that each time you use that docker module.

issue_title: We should test examples as part of system tests
issue_body:
Verifying examples work manually (read- we don't accidentally break them) is a hassle. We should integrate them to our nimi-bot testing.

First, the examples should be refactored so that they can be included – add `__name__`, main(), etc. similar to how [nixnet-python](https://github.com/ni/nixnet-python/blob/master/nixnet_examples/can_signal_conversion.py) does it.
(we should do this regardless).

Then we can have a single test file in each examples/ folder (note - this should not make it to documentation, will need filtering) that imports and invokes the examples in a way that they work on simulated device.

issue_title: `ModelTestCase.ensure_model_can_train_save_and_load` compares last-epoch weights with serialized *best* weights
issue_body:
in particular, if your test case model trained (say) 10 epochs, and got the best results in epoch 5, then the test would fail because it would compare `model.state_dict()` (i.e. epoch 10) with `archive.model.state_dict()` (the weights from epoch 5)

in the meantime I am setting `num_epoch = 1` as a workaround

issue_title: Option to predict tagger model without SpacyWordSplitter?
issue_body:
I am training a SimpleTagger instance and when trying to use the trained model to predict unseen data, I keep running into the problem that the output has more words than the gold. 
I looked into the code and instances of the class `sentence_tagger` always use the SpacyTokenizer (see here: https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/master/allennlp/service/predictors/sentence_tagger.py#L22). 

Since I am formatting the training data so that I have a `word###tag` format, I am assuming that the splitter is not applied here. Assuming that the test tgt is formatted in a spacy-compliant way seems like a pretty big assumption to me. 
Since you already have the JustSpaceTokenizer here (https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/master/allennlp/data/tokenizers/word_splitter.py#L128), I am wondering if there a method exists to tell the predictor to use that one (without monkeypatching the class on my end). 
If not, I suggest adding a tokenizer option to the config file.
