issue_title: README CI badge: explicitly mention branch
issue_body:
README shows ![build: passing](https://badgen.now.sh/badge/build/passing/green).

It refers to CI build of branch `develop`, but it doesn't explicitely says so, hence it can mislead user. For example, on a branch where CI does not pass, README will still show ![build: passing](https://badgen.now.sh/badge/build/passing/green).

The idea is to be more explicit by replacing keyword `build` in the badge, by the name of the branch whose status is referred to: ![develop: passing](https://badgen.now.sh/badge/develop/passing/green).

We have been using [img.shields.io](img.shields.io) for that purpose (see [this commit](https://github.com/gisaia/ARLAS-server/commit/d7f4d0c19a0c0778afa2418cf70d56d452e0a383)), but [it is under nearly permanent denial of service since its infrastructure is undersized](https://github.com/badges/shields/issues/1568), letting us no choice but to revert (see [this commit](https://github.com/gisaia/ARLAS-server/commit/b86a2b8f838744a7091cbfca830227dc07ed5313)).

[This comment](https://github.com/badges/shields/pull/1742#issuecomment-407446963) by [paulmelnikow](https://github.com/paulmelnikow), 2<sup>nd</sup> contributor on [badges/shields](https://github.com/badges/shields), mentions another service, [badgen](https://badgen.now.sh/), that apparently does not suffer from denial of service.

We should try using it.

Badges in this issue are generated using [https://badgen.now.sh/](https://badgen.now.sh/).

issue_title: TPOTBase.score doesn't impute values like TPOTBase.predict does
issue_body:
> TPOT's imputation step isn't stored in the internal pipelines, but it should apply imputation when you call any functions like fit, predict, score, etc.

Here's the code inside `TPOTBase(BaseEstimator).predict`

```
features = features.astype(np.float64)

if np.any(np.isnan(features)):
    self._imputed = True
    features = self._impute_values(features)
else:
    self._imputed = False
return self.fitted_pipeline_.predict(features)
```

So yeah, it will use the `self._fitted_imputer` when predicting because of the `self._impute_values` call.

But now here's the code inside `TPOTBase(BaseEstimator).score`:

```
score = SCORERS[self.scoring_function](
            self.fitted_pipeline_,
            testing_features.astype(np.float64),
            testing_target.astype(np.float64)
)
```

It simply forwards the `testing_features` without using the `self._fitted_imputer`, so unlike I expected, it won't do imputation automatically when using the `score` method.

issue_title: Transformation deserialization from a config file
issue_body:
I would like to know your opinion about the following code to add to torchvision. 
Suppose I have a configuration yaml file:
```yaml
"Compose":
    "transforms":
      - "ToThreeBands":
      - "ToPILImage":
      - "RandomApply":
          "p": 0.85
          "transforms":
            - "RandomAffine":
                "degrees": [-45, 45]
                "translate": [0.15, 0.15]
                "scale": [1.0, 1.2]
            - "RandomHorizontalFlip":
            - "RandomVerticalFlip":
      - "ToTensor":
      - "_Normalize":
```
that I would like to deserialize into `torchvision.transforms.transforms.Compose`.

The following can be done like this:
```python
from torchvision import transforms

_GLOBAL_PYTORCH_OBJECTS = dict([(name, cls) for name, cls in transforms.__dict__.items() if isinstance(cls, type)])

def deserialize(data, custom_objects=None):
    if isinstance(data, dict):        
        # Deserialize dictionary
        obj_dict = dict(data)
        for key in obj_dict:
            value = obj_dict[key]
            value = deserialize(value, custom_objects=custom_objects)
            if key in custom_objects:                    
                cls = custom_objects[key]
                return cls(**value) if value is not None else cls()
            obj_dict[key] = value
        return obj_dict            
    elif isinstance(data, (tuple, list)):
        # iterate over 
        return [deserialize(d, custom_objects=custom_objects) for d in data]
    else:
        return data

transforms_config = {....}  # Deserialized YAML

class ToThreeBands(object): pass
     #  My custom class to transform input images
     # ....

class _Normalize(object): pass
     #  My custom class to normalize tensors
     # ....

custom_objects = dict(_GLOBAL_PYTORCH_OBJECTS)
custom_objects.update({
    "ToThreeBands": ToThreeBands,
    "_Normalize": _Normalize
})

res = deserialize(transforms_config, custom_objects=custom_objects)
for t in res.transforms:
    print(type(t), t)
    if hasattr(t, "transforms"):
        for tt in t.transforms:
            print(type(tt), tt)
```
with the output:

Available streams: 540p_alt2, 720p_alt, 1080p_alt, 236p_alt, 360p_alt, 236p_alt2, 236p (worst),

issue_title: Duration is long now
Websockify behind one iptables bridge problem
issue_body:
Hello !

I've a working novnc + websockify service on a debian server.
When I'm connecting directly on the IP of the server over http://debian1:6080/ it's working perfectly.

However, when I'm using a debian bridge on my gateway ethernet link, it's no more working.
The debian iptables bridging is something I'm using since 20 years without any problem on TCP or UDP flow, it's the first time one TCP protocol is failing :)

Did someone have any idea why it's not working ? here are my firewall settings on the debian bridge who is filtering the internet paquets :


# Create special chain KEEP_STATE
iptables -N KEEP_STATE
iptables -F KEEP_STATE

# Drop bad states
iptables -A KEEP_STATE -m state --state INVALID -j DROP
iptables -A KEEP_STATE -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT

# Deny bad packets
iptables -A FORWARD -p tcp --tcp-flags ALL FIN,URG,PSH -m limit --limit 5/minute -j LOG --log-level notice --log-prefix "NMAP-XMAS: "
iptables -A FORWARD -p tcp --tcp-flags ALL FIN,URG,PSH -j DROP
iptables -A FORWARD -p tcp --tcp-flags SYN,FIN SYN,FIN -m limit --limit 5/minute -j LOG --log-level notice --log-prefix "SYN/FIN: "
iptables -A FORWARD -p tcp --tcp-flags SYN,FIN SYN,FIN -j DROP
iptables -A FORWARD -p tcp --tcp-flags SYN,RST SYN,RST -m limit --limit 5/minute -j LOG --log-level notice --log-prefix "SYN/RST: "
iptables -A FORWARD -p tcp --tcp-flags SYN,RST SYN,RST -j DROP

# Drop RST/ACKs to limit OS detection through pinging
iptables -A FORWARD -p tcp --tcp-flags RST RST,ACK -m limit --limit 5/minute -j LOG --log-level notice --log-prefix "RST/ACK: "
iptables -A FORWARD -p tcp --tcp-flags RST RST,ACK -j DROP

# Pass all boxes to the keep_state chain
iptables -A FORWARD -j KEEP_STATE

# Allow all outgoing traffic
iptables -A FORWARD -s 1.2.3.0/25 -j ACCEPT

# NoVNC WebSocket
iptables -A FORWARD -p tcp -d 1.2.3.9 --dport 6080 -j ACCEPT

# Set to drop all packets not accepted by rules above
iptables -A FORWARD -j DROP

issue_title: Reflecting trait changes in JavaScript back to Python
issue_body:
Hi all!

I've got a custom widget with some traits. This widget is basically a live stream, so I'm getting the data into JavaScript directly. I then programatically update a trait with `this.model.set('mytrait', some_value)`. This works great and triggers the corresponding `change:mytrait` event.

What I'd really like would be for a user in a notebook to be able to get the value of `mytrait` from Python. Ideally, this would only do the serialization when the user calls `widget.mytrait` in Python. Right now, if I do that, all I get is the initial default value set in Python (`None`).

I thought `this.touch()` or `this.model.save_changes()` would help, but they did not. So how can I set a model value programatically in JavaScript and have the user in Python able to get that value on demand?

issue_title: maxSurge should not be an integer field
Add aggregatable fields and index name to CSV Schema definition
issue_body:
[Moved from https://github.com/chaoss/grimoirelab-sigils/issues/144]

From https://github.com/chaoss/grimoirelab-sigils/issues/117:
>* CSV files needs a way to define when text fields should be aggregatable. By default all fields are aggregatable by default except text fields. I added a workaround considering that in our current CSVs, text implies being aggregatable. This should be removed when CSVs are improved.
>* CSV files are not specifying index names. As a workaround, I used file name without file extension for this.

issue_title: Non-zero exit status
issue_body:
If there were any modifications, it would be helpful if there was a non-zero status.

This would allow moban to be used in CI to detect (and usually fail) if all files were not in sync.

This could be done like `git diff --exit-code` which adds the exit code to reflect there were changes.

issue_title: sources.Sources does not support iteration
issue_body:
I'm fully aware now that probably the best way to iterate over all sources is `Sources.items()`, but when typing naïve code without reading the library source code, I ran into
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "../pylexirumah/clld_sqlite.py", line 197, in import_sources
    for source in wordlist.sources:
  File "/vol/home/kaipingga/devel/pycldf/pycldf/sources.py", line 106, in __getitem__
    return Source.from_entry(item, self._bibdata.entries[item])
  File "/vol/home/kaipingga/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pybtex/utils.py", line 154, in __getitem__
    return self._dict[key.lower()]
AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'lower'
```

That error is really confusing – The reason is that `Sources` implements a `__getitem__` method, but not `__iter__` and/or `__next__` (I don't actually know the lookup details here), which makes Python assume that it can iterate over this object by accessing `sources[0]` (and then presumably higher numbers until … what? IndexError? This Python implementation detail is strange, and too clever by half.)

issue_title: OAuth2 Authorization Code: OAuth2Error does not always include state
issue_body:
When raising OAuth2Errors, the state should be set so that it ends up in the redirect_uri (see [RFC 6749, Section 4.1.2.1](http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-4.1.2.1))

For example:

``` python
# oauth2/rfc6749/grant_types/authorization_code.py, Line 310:
if not self.request_validator.validate_response_type(request.client_id,
        request.response_type, request.client, request):
    log.debug('Client %s is not authorized to use response_type %s.',
              request.client_id, request.response_type)
    raise errors.UnauthorizedClientError(request=request)
```

This should either change to:

``` python
    raise errors.UnauthorizedClientError(state=request.state, request=request)
```

...or OAuth2Error should extract the state from the request automatically

``` python
# oauth2/rfc6749/errors.py, Line 46:
if request:
    self.redirect_uri = request.redirect_uri
    self.client_id = request.client_id
    self.scopes = request.scopes
    self.response_type = request.response_type
    self.grant_type = request.grant_type
    if not state:
        self.state = request.state   
```

issue_title: 请问有没有解决高并发时，access_token 重复请求的问题？
issue_body: 假设第一个请求（请求 A ）过来，程序发现 access_token 过期，这时就会去向微信服务器获取新的 access_token ，然后更新到全局变量中。这个过程本身没有问题，但是如果请求 A 在向微信服务器请求新的 access_token 期间，来了第二个请求（请求 B ），因为这时新的 access_token 还未更新到全局变量中，请求 B 就会认为 access_token 已过期，也会同请求 A 一样，去微信服务器请求新的 access_token ，这样就会导致请求 A 得到的 access_token 失效。想象一下，如果在请求 A 后面又来了 N 个请求，并且此时请求 A 还未成功更新 access_token ，那么后面的所有请求都会去微信服务器获取新的 access_token 。以上的情况，不仅会导致浪费带宽，而且会导致最后一个请求之前获取的 access_token 都失效。那我们应该如何做控制，在高并发情况下仅请求一次微信服务器呢？

issue_title: Bug: > should convert new line into white space
issue_body:
When using:

    accomplishment: >
      Mark set a major league
      home run record in 1998.

the result should be:

    { accomplishment: 'Mark set a major league home run record in 1998.' }

not:

    { accomplishment: 'Mark set a major leaguehome run record in 1998.' }

The > should convert new lines into white spaces according to the specification: http://www.yaml.org/spec/1.2/spec.html#id2760844

issue_title: EmbeddedModelField: Class name resolution doesn't work with abstract=True
Subscription add-ons' unit amounts should use the Subscription's currency
issue_body:
In an API response with a Subscription with a `subscription_add_ons` member, the add-ons' `unit_amount_in_cents` are modeled as `Money` instances. However, the matching `currency` for those Money instances is on the Subscription, not the add-on, so something goes awry and they can't be used.

The add-ons should fall back to using the Subscription's `currency` so the amounts are still available.
